channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Majority of Australians Across Generations Expect to Be Worse Off After Budget
item.description: A recent Newspoll has revealed widespread dissatisfaction with the Federal Budget, with Australians across all age groups expecting to be financially worse off. The poll shows Labor’s primary vote ste...

item.title: Michael Carrick set to clarify Manchester United managerial future after victory over Nottingham Forest
item.description: Michael Carrick is anticipating clarity on his future as Manchester United manager within the coming days. This statement followed United’s 3-2 victory against Nottingham Forest, a game in which Bruno...

item.title: Why Concert Ticket Sales Are Dropping in 2026
item.description: Following the post-pandemic surge in concert attendance, the music industry is now facing a phenomenon called 'blue dot fever,' named after unsold seats on Ticketmaster seating charts. After COVID-19 ...

item.title: Zilhaj Moon Sighting Confirms Eidul Azha on May 27 in Pakistan
item.description: The Ruet-i-Hilal Committee has officially announced that the Zilhaj moon has been sighted, confirming that Eidul Azha will be celebrated on Wednesday, May 27, 2026. The committee held its main meeting...

item.title: Shashi Tharoor humorously defends idli against rasgulla comparison
item.description: In a recent viral exchange on social media, Congress MP Shashi Tharoor responded with his signature wit to a post comparing the South Indian delicacy idli with the Bengali sweet rasgulla. The original...

item.title: Automakers Shift Jobs to Focus on AI Expertise Amid Workforce Changes
item.description: The automotive industry is experiencing a significant shift driven by artificial intelligence. Companies like General Motors have laid off a portion of their IT workforce—over 600 salaried employees—a...

item.title: Alberta lawyer maintains independence campaign despite court ruling
item.description: A recent Alberta court ruling has struck down a citizen-initiated petition for an independence referendum, citing a breach in the duty to consult with First Nations. Justice Shaina Leonard of the Albe...

item.title: Shohei Ohtani drives in five runs as Dodgers defeat Angels decisively
item.description: Shohei Ohtani delivered a standout performance as the Los Angeles Dodgers routed the Los Angeles Angels, recording a season-high five RBIs. The two-way star, known for both his pitching and hitting, c...

item.title: Virat Kohli open to 2027 ODI World Cup if he can meaningfully contribute
item.description: India cricket legend Virat Kohli, now 37, has indicated that he will consider playing in the 2027 ODI World Cup only if he believes he can meaningfully contribute to the team. Kohli, who retired from ...

item.title: Three Killed and Several Injured in Oakland Pedestrian Crash
item.description: Three people were killed and several others injured late Saturday night when a speeding vehicle struck pedestrians at the intersection of 85th Avenue and International Boulevard in Oakland. The crash ...

item.title: Ukraine launches drone strike on Moscow following Russian missile attack on Kyiv
item.description: Overnight, a Ukrainian drone strike in the Moscow region resulted in the deaths of at least three people and injured a dozen others, according to Russian authorities. The attack occurred in the afterm...

item.title: National University beats La Salle in FilOil preseason basketball opener
item.description: In a surprising start to the FilOil preseason basketball tournament, National University (NU) managed to upset the defending champions, De La Salle University (DLSU). The game, held on May 17, 2026, s...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Holds Talk with Qatari PM on Regional Peace Efforts
item.description: On May 17, 2026, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif held a phone conversation with Qatari Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim al Thani, appreciating Qatar's support for Pakistan-led i...

item.title: Lynn Redgrave: A Portrait of Her Early Career and Style in the 1960s
item.description: Lynn Redgrave, born on March 8, 1943, emerged in the 1960s as a unique and unconventional presence in British cinema and theatre. While her sister Vanessa embodied classical beauty, Lynn became the fa...

item.title: TTV Dhinakaran threatens disqualification of MLA S Kamaraj for backing TVK in Tamil Nadu floor test
item.description: In a recent political development in Tamil Nadu, TTV Dhinakaran, chief of Amma Makkal Munnetra Kazhagam (AMMK), has threatened disqualification of party MLA S Kamaraj after he extended support to the ...

item.title: Surge in American applications for Canadian citizenship proof leads to longer processing delays
item.description: According to the Canadian government’s processing-time estimator, applicants for Canadian citizenship certificates are now facing waits of up to a year due to a significant increase in applications fr...

item.title: Tanzania and Russia Formalize Strategic Agreements
item.description: Tanzania na Russia wameingia mkatano muhimu wa kushirikiana, hatua inayoonyesha ushirikiano unaokua baina ya mataifa haya mawili. Mikatano hii inahusisha sekta mbalimbali zikiwemo biashara, uwekezaji ...

item.title: Oburu Odinga to Engage Government on Soaring Fuel Prices and Cost of Living
item.description: Kisumu – ODM leader Oburu Odinga has expressed serious concern over the recent spike in fuel prices, acknowledging the heavy economic strain on Kenyan households. During a Luo Nyanza delegates’ meetin...

item.title: Chelsea Fans Express Frustration as Team Struggles Under Boehly Ownership
item.description: Since Todd Boehly and the BlueCo consortium took over Chelsea in May 2022, expectations were high that the club would invest heavily and maintain its status at the top of English football. However, fo...

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a public health emergency
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern. The outbreak, caused by...
